title: Battery Management, Mid-Ride Motor Cutoff Safety, and Vehicle Damage Liability
summary: Council questions on practical operations: battery range (45 miles full charge, swap every 10 rides, removed from app at 5%), low-funds notification (verbal alert from bike, $20 pre-authorization to prevent mid-ride cutoff). Council member reports witnessing near-accident when bike motor cut off mid-ride near a bus -- Veo clarifies motor disengages but does not apply brakes. Damage liability: rider bears responsibility; Veo tracks all ride paths via 4-second GPS pings but legally requires subpoena for personally identifiable data (even for SPD). Council member notes bikes left on lawns remain biggest constituent complaint -- Council President Paniagua had one for 4 days. Virtual parking coach penalty: meter runs up to 20 minutes if rider ignores parking instructions. Charging racks technically possible but require grid tie-in; swappable batteries preferred for operational flexibility. DPW partnership sought for additional bike rack deployment.

political_signal.description: Council pressing on mid-ride safety incidents, damage liability processes, and persistent parking/lawn complaints from constituents

processing_notes: Law Department in detected_departments is from legal discussion of subpoena requirements; SPD and DPW are the substantively referenced departments
$6 and $20 amounts relate to pre-authorization discussion, not contract costs
Mid-ride motor cutoff incident: motor disengages but does not brake -- safety concern raised by council
Segment 4 of 6 from Public Transportation Committee Meeting, Tuesday, February 4th, 2026
Transcript corrections applied: SU -> Syracuse University
Potential duplicate detected: overlaps with CTX-COUNCIL-2026-00026 -- linked, not skipped
